ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ki Yisa betɔ a “N sa saan ki saa te wun paak.”
translated: He said to them, "I will not go until you all come."

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Ki Yisa lek ki fiin ba yet linba na, ŋaan bet saakɔɔ na a “Daa tiin jaŋmaanii. Teentin yada kuukɔɔ.”
translated: When Yisa saw that he was about to die, he said to his son, "Take care of my wife. Bury me properly."

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Li taakpaak ni ki bonpobik na fiir set kakibii, ki somm lin. Ki li yaar ŋaan litib bonchiann.
translated: The woman who is going to the farm is carrying a basket of shea nuts, she is going to sell them. She is going to buy some food.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ki leer mun din baa tanpuut paak, siaminba ki tant ki yabi, ki li din pia ki kpaat yiama, kimaan tant ki yab leŋi,
translated: If you see a person who is doing something wrong, tell him to stop, because it is not good to do bad things, and if he refuses to listen, leave him alone.

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Yisa nba din nyii Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni ki saa na, ki u poorpoweiteeb din baar u boor ni ki tan wantɔ jiantu ŋaak maŋ diit.
translated: Yisa told Yennu that he should not be afraid, because he will help him, and he will give him a good wife.
